threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look bewildered when the bus driver shouts 'Next stop, get off!' like it’s a personal attack.
Caught a glimpse of a sleeping man on the 35 last night, cradling a half-eaten pepperoni pizza like it was a baby. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found out my favorite bakery was open at 6 AM, just as a group of night owls wrapped up their evening. That’s Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a 24/7 Döner place where the line is always longer than at the club around the corner. Late-night cravings sorted!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only true locals will know to avoid BART on rainy days; they don't just bring umbrellas, they bring snacks for the inevitable delays.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni stuck in the Civic Center loop yesterday. A dog on the tracks and a saxophonist jamming nearby made for an oddly charming chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real gem is the small artists' ateliers tucked away in corners, each with a story to tell.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewildered look at the metro ticket machines—totally lost in translation with the options.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of 'the Underground'—everyone here knows it's a London thing, not a tourist thing.
Saw a guy in a suit on the 59 last night, nodding off with a pizza box in his lap. When he jolted awake, he nearly sent it flying!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Last week, I spotted a street musician playing classical opera on a trumpet while a group of kids danced around him, totally capturing the Neukölln spirit.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see a crowd waiting at a tram stop while the bus zips by empty. It's like a public transport dance-off every morning.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only real locals know to carry an umbrella in November but leave it at home in February. The weather's as moody as our coffee preferences.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading commuters at the Civic Center station, pausing only for the deafening train horn. Everyone just kept scrolling on their phones.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all hip cafes and bars, but the real gems are the little bakeries that open at dawn – best croissants you’ll ever have.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still carry a map instead of checking their phone. You can see the confusion when they stare at the street signs with the wrong pronunciations.
